question_frequency.rationale: The Pair never poses a didactic question. It delivers a complete solution as 'A:' (no B option, despite the project's two-option premise) and only at the very end offers generic follow-up menu items (add tests? adjust contractions?), which are not teachable-moment prompts.

question_quality.rationale: No real choice is framed. Genuine forks — contractions as one word vs. split, Unicode letters vs. ASCII-only, returning Counter vs. dict — are mentioned as asides in prose rather than surfaced as decisions for the user to make.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no progression to scaffold; the entire solution is dumped in one turn with explanation appended. The user never gets to attempt, predict, or decide anything.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and friendly and the explanation is competent, but it's a monologue. The 'final' turn is the user (a Haiku model) merely validating the solution, so there's no genuine back-and-forth.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
